name: Schwab v. Reilly
question:
1) When a debtor claims an item "exempt" from bankruptcy proceedings, must an interested party object to the claimed exemption in order to preserve their right to claim any value in the item exceeding the value listed as exempt?

2) Does a creditor who wishes to sell a debtor's "exempted" item need to timely object to its exempt status in order to move for its sale, even if its real value exceeds the value claimed by the debtor?
description: A case in which the Court held that someone who files for bankruptcy is allowed to retain the value of certain property that would otherwise be sold to pay off debts, up to a certain dollar limit; if the value of the property is more than the individual in bankruptcy estimated, the value above the pre-determined limit may be used to pay creditors.
